detected_errors: The topology starts with [ molecules ] SOL 0 and then relies on gmx solvate -p to update it; this is usually workable, but the workflow should explicitly verify and correct the final SOL count after solvation.
The validated RDF command uses -sel 'name HW', but common SPC/E water files use HW1 and HW2, so the command may fail unless atom names are checked first.

The default O-H RDF may include intramolecular covalent O-H correlations; the workflow correctly mentions -excl, but the recommended final command still reports the non-excluded RDF without clearly making intermolecular RDF the preferred structural result.
correction_suggestions: After gmx solvate -p topol.top, explicitly check that the [ molecules ] section contains the correct SOL number and not SOL 0.
Use the atom-name-safe RDF command as the main recommendation: gmx rdf -f md.xtc -s md.tpr -o rdf_OW_HW.xvg -ref 'name OW' -sel 'name HW1 HW2' for SPC/E, or adjust according to md.gro.
For physical interpretation of liquid-water O-H structure, make the -excl version the preferred final RDF command and state that the non-excluded RDF includes intramolecular O-H peaks.
For publication-quality RDF, recommend longer production MD and block/convergence checking of RDF curves.
